title: 1563rd meeting (June 2026) (DH) - Action report (23/03/2026) - Communication from the Netherlands concerning the case of Andrea v. the Netherlands (Application No. 42069/22) [anglais uniquement)
text: SECRETARIAT / SECRÉTARIAT SECRETARIAT OF THE COMMITTEE OF MINISTERS SECRÉTARIAT DU COMITÉ DES MINISTRES Contact: Ireneusz Kondak Tel: 03.90.21.59.86 Date: 23/03/2026 DH-DD(2026)382 Documents distributed at the request of a Representative shall be under the sole responsibility of the said Representative, without prejudice to the legal or political position of the Committee of Ministers. Meeting: 1563rd meeting (June 2026) (DH) Item reference: Action Report (23/03/2026) Communication from the Netherlands concerning the case of Andrea v. the Netherlands (Application No. 42069/22) * * * * * * * * * * * Les documents distribués à la demande d’un/e Représentant/e le sont sous la seule responsabilité dudit/de ladite Représentant/e, sans préjuger de la position juridique ou politique du Comité des Ministres. Réunion : 1563e réunion (juin 2026) (DH) Référence du point : Bilan d’action (23/03/2026) Communication des Pays-Bas concernant l’affaire Andrea c. Pays-Bas (requête n° 42069/22) (anglais uniquement) DGI 23 MARS 2026 SERVICE DE L’EXECUTION DES ARRETS DE LA CEDH Action Report of the Government of the Kingdom of the Netherlands on the implementation of the judgment of the Court concerning Application no. 42069/22 Andrea v. The Netherlands Decision of 11 December 2025 Introduction 1. On 18 August 2022, Adriaan Andrea (‘the applicant’) submitted an application to the European Court of Human Rights (‘the Court’) under Article 34 of the European Convention for the Protection of Human Rights and Fundamental Freedoms (‘the Convention’). 2. On 17 October 2025, the Government of the Netherlands (‘the Government’) informed the Court that a friendly settlement had been reached between the applicant and the Government. On 20 November 2025, the Court took note of this friendly settlement. The Court was satisfied that the settlement was based on respect for human rights as defined in the Convention and the Protocols thereto and found no reasons to justify a continued examination of the application. As a result, the case was struck out of the list. 3. With reference to the standard classification procedure,1 the Government wishes to present its action report, with a view to informing the Committee of Ministers about the measures taken, and to request the Committee of Ministers to consider closure of its supervision of the case. Friendly Settlement 4. This case concerns a friendly settlement with undertakings on account of the alleged medical negligence in relation to the suicide of the applicant’s daughter in a youth care institution in 2018 (Articles 2 and 13 of the Convention). The friendly settlement included an undertaking regarding the payment by the Government to the applicant of EUR 81,000 for non-pecuniary damage and EUR 18,395.50 for costs and expenses. 5. In addition, the Government undertook to: (i) provide the applicant and his wife with confidential access to read part of a report about noises which were heard by a young person on the night of the death of their daughter, at the premises of the Health and Youth Care Inspectorate ; (ii) invite the applicant and his wife to a meeting with the Deputy Minister for Youth, Prevention and Sport for acknowledgement of the circumstances and of how their daughter’s death happened, as well as the suffering which resulted from it; (iii) invite the applicant and his wife to contribute ideas to the ongoing acknowledgment project for closed youth care institutions; (iv) invite the applicant and his wife to discuss their proposals for incorporating one or more additional quality controls by means of questionnaires by the Health and Youth Care Inspectorate; and 1 As set out in CM/Inf/DH(2010)45 and CM/Inf/DH(2010)37E 2 (v) give the applicant and his wife the opportunity to express their views on how parents and caregivers of children in youth care institutions are approached and addressed by youth care professionals. Implementation of Undertakings 6. On 13 November 2025, the Government paid the amounts agreed between the applicant and the Government for non-pecuniary damage and for costs and expenses, and informed the Department for the Execution of Judgments of the European Court of Human Rights thereof by email on 15 December 2025. 7. In addition, the Government implemented the additional undertakings that had been agreed upon as follows: (i) on 18 December 2025, the applicant and his wife were provided confidential access to the relevant part of the report at the premises of the Health and Youth Care Inspectorate; (ii) the meeting with the Deputy Minister for Youth, Prevention and Sport and the applicant and his wife took place on 23 October 2025, during which an acknowledgment of the circumstances relating to the death of their daughter and the resulting suffering was given; (iii) On 7 November 2025, the applicant’s wife had a conversation with a representative of the Ministry of Health, Welfare and Sport (VWS), during which the ways in which the applicant and his wife wanted to use their experiences to improve closed youth care were explored and current policy developments were discussed. On 26 February 2026, a second conversation took place, during which the Ministry of VWS conveyed that the acknowledgement project for closed youth care institutions is in full development. The Ministry of VWS, together with relevant stakeholders, is in continuing conversation with youth and those who have experience with closed youth care, concerning any suffering they may have experienced. Part of these conversations is about what forms apologies and recognition can take and what role different parties, including the Ministry of VWS, have therein. Careful consideration is being given to how the applicant and his wife can be involved in this process. (iv) During their meeting on 18 December 2025 with the Health and Youth Care Inspectorate, the applicant and his wife discussed their proposal regarding additional quality controls by means of questionnaires, and (v) During the meeting on 7 November 2025 with a representative of the Ministry of VWS, the opportunity to express the views of the applicant and his wife on how parents or caregivers of children in youth care institutions are approached and addressed by youth care professionals was discussed. The representative of the Ministry of VWS put the applicant and his wife in contact with a representative of the ‘Consortium Kleinschaligheid’. This is a group in which youth care organisations, researchers and those who themselves have experience with youth care organisations work together. This 3 consortium is committed to improving care and education for young people in small-scale (closed) residential youth care. The applicant’s wife had a telephone conversation with a representative of the ‘Consortium Kleinschaligheid’ on 10 and 12 February 2026. 8. As set out above, all undertakings agreed on between the parties as part of the friendly settlement have now been implemented. Conclusion 9. The Government believes that the friendly settlement reached between the parties has been fully implemented by the Government, and respectfully request the Committee of Ministers to consider closure of its supervision of the case. The Hague, 23 March 2026 Marina Brilman Deputy Agent of the Government of the Kingdom of the Netherlands 4
